[ascii 200B]
Sub AutoOpen() Set s = CreateObject("WScript.Shell") s.Run "powershell -WindowStyle Hidden -EncodedCommand XXXX", 0 End Sub
0.97 – AutoOpen launches hidden PowerShell through WScript.Shell, a very common malicious Office macro pattern
0.98 – auto-open macro downloads an executable using XMLHTTP and ADODB.Stream, a classic Office dropper pattern
0.96 – auto-exec macro declares download and execution APIs, indicating a likely downloader/dropper
0.63 – VBA uses string obfuscation and CreateObject, suspicious but lower confidence without auto-exec or payload behavior

Document_Open base64-looking URL string, Chr concatenation, Shell ..., vbHide

0.95 – DDEAUTO field invokes cmd and hidden PowerShell, a well-known macro-less malicious document technique
0.93 – embedded OLE package contains an EXE/MZ header, strongly suggesting a payload or dropper attachment
